original_hypothesis.title: PriceOracle lacks time-based staleness bounds, enabling long-lived stale pricing on Chainlink outages
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: PriceOracle.getAssetPriceFromChainlink() validates updatedAt != 0 and answeredInRound >= roundId but does not enforce a max age. If an aggregator halts updates, the protocol can keep accepting the last price indefinitely, allowing users to borrow against overvalued collateral after real market moves. This is a protocol-critical oracle freshness gap.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Stale-oracle events happen in production (heartbeat failures, sequencer issues, feed pausing). Without a max-age check, lending invariants become dependent on uninterrupted oracle operation.
deployment_assumptions: At least one listed ERC20 uses Chainlink pricing via assetChainlinkAggregators
Chainlink feed can become stale while still returning the last round data
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: InitConfigPool.s.sol sets Chainlink aggregators for WETH/USDT/DAI; PriceOracle.getAssetPriceFromChainlink has no 'block.timestamp - updatedAt' check.

proof_of_signal.pattern: staleness checks omit time threshold: require(updatedAt != 0) and answeredInRound>=roundId only

preconditions: Chainlink feed stops updating while keeping last valid round retrievable
Protocol continues allowing borrow/withdraw/liquidation decisions using this oracle
Attacker can act after observing stale state on-chain (no mempool needed)

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: accepts arbitrarily old prices during outages

analysis_questions: Which protocol operations rely on PriceOracle for collateral/debt valuation (borrow limits, liquidation triggers)?
Are there any emergency pause hooks triggered by stale prices?
Is there any external monitoring that pauses markets during oracle issues?
validation_requirements: Fork test: freeze Chainlink feed (mock updatedAt far in past) and verify borrows still allowed
Confirm liquidation/health factor computations use this oracle output directly
Design check: define acceptable staleness threshold per asset

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: During an oracle outage, attacker borrows against collateral valued at outdated higher price; when oracle resumes, position is underwater leaving bad debt.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Potentially large bad debt across pools during major market moves
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All lenders in markets priced by affected feeds
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Partial via liquidation if collateral remains; otherwise bad debt

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Monitor updatedAt age for feeds; block borrowing when exceeds threshold

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
